genre_refs: retro/vaporwave Americana aesthetic, vintage California convertible road photo
description: A nostalgic, retro-styled slogan graphic built on a warm, sun-faded vintage photograph shot from behind a teal/aqua 1960s convertible cruising down a palm-tree-lined street toward distant hills (evoking classic Southern California / Hollywood Hills). The backs of two passengers' heads (a dark-haired person and a blonde) are visible in the foreground; vintage cars line the curb. The image has heavy warm color grading, soft grain and a sun-flared, dreamy aesthetic. A small circular Department/agency seal sits centered. Overlaid across the middle in a flowing white script font is the slogan 'Recapture the Dream'. No other body text appears on the image. The messaging is purely aspirational and emotional, tying the 'American Dream' to a romanticized mid-century image of prosperity; per the tweet it accompanies a message about reversing 'America Last, globalist policies' to 'Recapture the American Dream for the AMERICAN PEOPLE.' Propaganda technique: nostalgia and emotional appeal to a lost golden age. Target audience: working- and middle-class Americans, an 'America First' base.
